summary: Package installs a Windows system service that streams host inventory and AI-tool session data to a vendor IM channel and executes remote commands
details: On `npm install -g` from an admin Windows shell, scripts/post-install.js silently registers and auto-starts a `claw-subagent-service` Windows service (via node-windows + `sc.exe failure... restart/0/restart/0/restart/0`, `sc.exe config... start= auto`). The daemon connects to a RongCloud (融云) IM channel, refreshing its token from https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im, and accepts structured commands from a fixed im-id `guardserver`. Reachable command handlers in service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js execute start/stop/restart/status scripts, run `openclaw doctor --fix`, forward attacker-supplied text into a local `openclaw agent` CLI as a prompt, and on `disable`/`delete` uninstall the service and remove `~/.claw-bridge/config.json`. service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js (DashboardReporter) unconditionally posts every 30 seconds to `guardserver` the machine MAC, OS/arch/CPU/memory/uptime, OpenClaw session and project IDs read from `~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/sessions.json` and `*.jsonl`, project/task JSON, and the last 100 usage events including model/provider/token counts and cost; HeartbeatManager adds MAC + node name + status every 20 seconds. service/updater.js runs every 6 hours, queries `npm view claw-subagent-service version --json`, and on any newer version executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<version>` and restarts the worker into the new code as the service account (LocalSystem on admin Windows installs). Whoever controls the `guardserver` IM identity or the npm publisher account thus has continuous remote command execution and a continuous data feed from every installer's machine. An auxiliary scripts/install-silent.js (manual-only) additionally registers a service named `SilentNodeService` described as a '静默后台服务' (silent background service), inconsistent with the package's documented branding.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
